title: Executive Order 11832—Establishing a National Commission on the Observance of International Women's Year, 1975

flags.justification: A purely advisory, promotional body (up to 35 members) with no operational or regulatory authority.
Sec. 2(h) sets a clear structure: conclude work by end of 1975, report within thirty days, then terminate.
Multiple related functions (promotion, action-agenda items, liaison, encouraging private-sector participation) all coherently serve one purpose.
A purely advisory body with no compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
Sec. 2(h) sets an explicit, hard termination trigger: conclude by end of 1975, report within thirty days, 'The Commission shall then be terminated.'
No existing oversight mechanism is removed; this creates a new advisory function.
An advisory body with independent, private-citizen membership; no ongoing third-party compliance regime is created.
A time-limited, task-specific commission implementing a UN-designated observance year; no ongoing authority is claimed.
Sec. 4's interdepartmental task force is coordination, not mandate redirection.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/14 = 0.0%. Another exemplary time-limited independent advisory commission, joining EO 9387, EO 9808, and EO 11076 as clean designs of this type.
